**Theorem 1.** For a link $L$, $\pi(\mathbb{R}^3 - L)$ is generated by $\{\bar{g}_1, \bar{g}_2, \ldots, \bar{g}_n\}$, where each $g_i$ is a geometric triangle looping around an arc of the diagram. That is, every element can be expressed as a product $\bar{p} = \prod_{i=1}^{m} \bar{g}_{j_i}^{\alpha_i}$ ($\alpha_i = \pm 1$).
